market_summary: S&P 500 hit all-time highs this week, closing at 7,041.28 on April 17 (first-ever close above 7,000 in history) and surging a further ~1.3% on April 18 as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open.' Estimated April 18 close ~7,133. Weekly S&P gain ~+4.63% (from April 11 baseline ~6,817). Nasdaq surged +5.2% and notched its 12th consecutive positive session — longest win streak since 2009. The Hormuz reopening caused oil to drop 12% and equities to extend gains. US naval blockade technically remains until a full formal peace deal; Trump said a deal is expected 'in days.' Portfolio NAV adjusted to $4,314.87 after NVDA 205C call option expired worthless on April 17 ($8.50 total loss accepted).
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Strait of Hormuz declared 'completely open' by Iran on April 18 — the single most significant geopolitical development of the week. Oil dropped 12%. US blockade remains until formal peace deal but Trump expects agreement within days. Full resolution will trigger GLD invalidation condition review (4+ week sustained risk-on requirement). 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — driven by a 21.2% monthly gasoline surge (largest ever recorded), directly caused by Iran war Hormuz disruption. Core CPI +2.6% YoY. With Hormuz now opening, analysts project CPI peaks near 4% then declines toward 3% by year-end — energy shock reversal is now in motion. 3. FED: Rate held at 3.50-3.75%. FOMC April 28-29 is the next critical catalyst. Dot plot signals one cut in 2026; markets price two (50bps total). Ceasefire and declining energy inflation increase probability of H2 2026 cuts. 4. GOLD: XAU/USD rose to ~$4,878 on April 18 — counterintuitively UP despite Hormuz opening, as lower inflation reduces tightening need (gold positive). Institutional targets surged dramatically: JPM $6,300, UBS $6,200 (mid-2026), Goldman Sachs $5,400. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Broad 2026 YTD trend is value over tech (Energy +21-25% YTD, Materials +18%, Staples +16%). This specific week saw tech lead again with Nasdaq +5.2%. Energy now taking a hit on Hormuz reopening (oil -12%). 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated rates (ex-China 125%) continues. Business investment still suppressed by policy uncertainty. US average effective tariff rate in double digits.
portfolio_health: Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,314.87 (adjusted for expired NVDA 205C option) — up +$88.94 (+2.11%) from April 11 Friday baseline of $4,225.93, and +$123.87 (+2.96%) YTD from April 4 seed of $4,191.00. Weekly return +2.11% vs S&P 500 estimated +4.63% — underperformance of 2.52pp entirely attributable to 12.57% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (25.84% of NAV vs 36% target = -10.16pp gap). The CSPX $250 ADD is now in its SEVENTH consecutive weekly report without execution — this is the portfolio's singular operational failure and it has cost measurable compounding returns as the S&P has surged to all-time highs. NVDA 205C call option confirmed expired worthless April 17 — $8.50 total loss absorbed. WHT structure remains clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD (all from MA dividend). Total unrealized P&L on equity/ETF positions: +$209.22. ASML is the weakest position: barely above cost (+0.98%), facing DUV restriction bill risk and China mix compression. All other equity positions are positive with intact or strengthening theses.

positions.rationale: S&P 500 at all-time highs (~7,133 on April 18), Nasdaq on record 12-day win streak, risk-on fully restored after Hormuz reopening. CSPX is 25.84% of NAV vs 36% target — 10.16pp structural gap. ADD $250 immediately at Monday open (April 20). This is the 7th consecutive report issuing this recommendation. No further deferral justifiable under any market conditions.

IE-domiciled accumulating structure remains optimal — 0% WHT via Irish fund portfolio interest exemption. YTM 3.68% remains above the 3.0% trim trigger. Stage-down from ~14% to ~10% of NAV formally initiated but contingent on CSPX ADD executing first and FOMC April 28-29 guidance. Do not trim before the CSPX gap is meaningfully closed.

AWS backlog $244B (+40% YoY), AI run rate $15B, custom silicon (Graviton + Trainium) exceeding $10B annualized run rate with >50% YoY growth. Q1 2026 earnings April 29 — 11 days away. Analyst consensus 43/43 Strong Buy, $281 average target (+12% upside from $249.70). Position +16.71% unrealized. No reason to trade ahead of a major catalyst. Thesis STRONGER.

Gold rose to ~$4,878 on April 18 despite Hormuz opening — ceasefire reduces inflation, which limits tightening need (net gold positive). Institutional targets surged: JPM $6,300, UBS $6,200, Goldman $5,400. Invalidation condition requires 4+ weeks of sustained risk-on post-full resolution — not yet met. Price alert at GLD $420 remains active (current $440.08 well above trigger). Thesis STRONGER. Flag: WATCH on Hormuz durability.

GTC 2026 $1T confirmed purchase order backlog through 2027. Q4 FY2026 record $68.1B revenue (+73% YoY). Q1 FY2027 guidance $78B. Stock +18% over 10 days — elevated post-surge. Analyst consensus 39 Strong Buy, $275 median target (+39% upside from $198.35). Do not add at current elevated levels; let compound. Reassess on pullback to $180-185. Next earnings ~May 20. Thesis STRONGLY STRONGER.

35/38 analysts rate Buy/Outperform, $655 average target (+26% upside). EARNINGS DATE DISCREPANCY: some sources cite April 23 (this Wednesday), others April 30. Must verify immediately. Reserve $100 cash for ADD on earnings beat (cross-border volume >14%, VAS >20%, confident FY guidance). WHT on dividend <$1/year — immaterial. Thesis SAME pending earnings.

positions.wht_exposure: 15% assumed (unverified — may be 30% US NRA; ACTION REQUIRED)
positions.rationale: Q1 2026 beat (EUR 8.77B revenue, EUR 2.76B net income) and guidance raised to EUR 36-40B confirms EUV monopoly intact. HOWEVER: China mix fell 36% to 19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold); new US bipartisan bill proposes banning DUV sales to China — a significant new risk beyond existing EUV controls. Stock barely above cost (+0.98%); Jefferies flagging valuation de-rate. Too small ($245.34) to trim economically at current commission levels. Monitor closely. Thesis WEAKER — Flag: WATCH/DETERIORATING.

rebalancing: Single clear action drives the entire rebalancing decision: ADD $250 to CSPX immediately (7th consecutive report, non-negotiable). Post-buy: CSPX rises from 25.84% to ~31.6% of NAV; cash falls from 12.57% to ~6.8%. Remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$292): reserve $100 for MA earnings add (April 23 or 30 — verify date); deploy $192 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline (15 days). U03A stage-down (14% to 10% of NAV) is on track — execute contingent on FOMC April 28-29 guidance and CSPX gap closing. No other positions require rebalancing. ASML is too small to trim economically despite WEAKER flag.
cash_strategy: $542.41 available (12.57% of NAV) — this is structural drag, not dry powder. Step 1 (Monday April 20): Deploy $250 into CSPX. Post-trade cash $292.41 (6.78% of NAV). Step 2 (April 23 or 30, MA earnings): ADD $100 into MA contingent on earnings beat. Post-trade cash ~$192. Step 3 (May 3 hard deadline — 15 days): Deploy remaining ~$192 into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum operating buffer at all times. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3 under any circumstances.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom: highest-conviction new-position candidate. AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator programs), networking dominance via VMware integration. Shares up 50%+ YTD in 2026; named by BofA as one of 6 stocks leading the $1T chip surge. Zero effective dividend = 0% WHT. Complements NVDA as second AI semiconductor name. DO NOT BUY NOW — close CSPX gap and deploy Tranche 3 first. Revisit when NAV > $5,000.

Meta Platforms: zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI infrastructure capex, Llama open-source ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet. WhatsApp Business and Reality Labs AI monetization accelerating. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap fully closed.

action_items: CRITICAL IMMEDIATE (Monday April 20 open): Buy CSPX $250 (~0.33 units at ~$756-770). SEVENTH consecutive report. S&P 500 at all-time highs, Hormuz open, risk-on fully restored. No further deferral under any circumstances.
URGENT (today April 18): Verify MA Q1 2026 earnings date — April 23 or April 30? Conflicting sources. Prepare ADD $100 decision for earnings beat scenario. Set calendar alert for the correct date.
April 20 (Monday): Confirm NVDA 205C option shows as expired/closed in IBKR with $8.50 total loss fully absorbed.
April 23 or April 30 — MA earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% and VAS growth >20% with confident full-year guidance. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
April 28-29 — FOMC: Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish signal or explicit rate cut = trigger U03A stage-down from ~14% toward 10% of NAV (redeploy into CSPX).
April 29 — AMZN Q1 earnings: Monitor AWS revenue, operating margin, and AI monetization. Beat expected; no pre-earnings action required.
May 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$192 post-CSPX-and-MA) into CSPX unconditionally. No extensions.

ONGOING — ASML WHT: Verify on next IBKR tax document whether 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate is applied. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to AMS-listed ASML shares to eliminate US NRA withholding.
WATCH — GLD: Monitor Iran ceasefire durability. Full US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz formally reopened + 4+ weeks sustained risk-on = trim 25% of GLD position and redeploy into CSPX.
WATCH — ASML: If China mix falls below 15% of net sales in Q2 2026 OR DUV restriction bill advances materially in Congress, initiate partial trim and redeploy into CSPX.
